options.name: -help
options.description: Prints out the command usage

options.name: <object_list>
options.description: Specifies the list of instances, library cells, or nets to which the derating will be applied

options.name: <derate_value>
options.description: Specifies the derating value to be used

options.name: -add
options.description: Incrementally adds or subtracts derates

options.name: -cell_check
options.description: Specifies scaling on timing checks

options.name: -cell_delay
options.description: Specifies scaling on cell delays

options.name: -clock
options.description: Applies the derating factors to clock paths only

options.name: -clock_period_check
options.description: Specifies derate values for clock period checks

options.name: -corner
options.description: Applies derating on cells/nets which are modeled as corner data elements

options.name: -data
options.description: Applies the derating factors to data paths only

options.name: -delay_corner
options.description: Applies the derating factor to the specified delay calculation corners

options.name: -dynamic
options.description: Applies the specified derating factor to the dynamic portion of cell/net delays

options.name: -early
options.description: Specifies that the derating factor applies to early paths

options.name: -fall
options.description: Applies the derating factor to the falling edge of the paths

options.name: -increment
options.description: incrementally add the derate value to total derate value in all (OCV/AOCV/SOCV) mode.

options.name: -input_switching
options.description: Understand the given derate as Simultaneous Switching Input derate applicable to early paths only

options.name: -late
options.description: Specifies that the derating factor applies to late paths

options.name: -max
options.description: Applies the derating factor to the maximum operating condition during best-case worst-case analysis

options.name: -mean
options.description: Used in the SOCV analysis flow to specify a derating factor for the mean component of delay. For cell delays and checks, -mean is supported on library_cell and instance objects. For net delays, it is supported as a global-level derate factor. The -multiply and -add options can be used with -mean to provide an incremental adjustment to a previously specified -mean base derate.

options.name: -min
options.description: Applies the derating factor to the minimum operating condition during best-case worst-case analysis

options.name: -multiply
options.description: Incrementally multiplies derates

options.name: -net_delay
options.description: Specifies scaling on net delays

options.name: -power_domain
options.description: Allows you to specify a specific power domain for the timing derate factors to be applied

options.name: -pulse_width_check
options.description: Specifies derate values for pulse width checks

options.name: -retain_delay
options.description: Specifies scaling on retain constructs

options.name: -rise
options.description: Applies the derating factor to the rising edge of the paths

options.name: -sigma
options.description: Used in the SOCV analysis flow to specify a derating factor for the variation component of delay. For cell delays and checks, -sigma is supported on library_cell and instance objects. For net delays, it is supported as a global-level derate factor. The -multiply and -add options can be used with -sigma to provide an incremental adjustment to a previously specified -sigma base derate.

options.name: -static
options.description: Applies the specified derating factor to the static portion of cell/net delays

options.name: -statistical
options.description: Applies derating on cells/nets which are modeled as statistical data elements

options.name: -transition
options.description: Specifies scaling on transitions
